evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my .NET for Android app. Here's my empty Activity: EmptyActivity.cs. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit NuGet versions, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration code into EmptyActivity.cs (BarcodeCapture.Create, IBarcodeCaptureListener implementation, Camera, DataCaptureView, BarcodeCaptureOverlay, OnResume/OnPause lifecycle), and shows the setup checklist (NuGet packages, AndroidManifest permissions, runtime permission, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding the Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages
Setup checklist mentions adding android.permission.CAMERA to AndroidManifest.xml
Setup checklist mentions requesting the CAMERA permission at runtime
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
BarcodeCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings is used (as a property, not VideoResolution.Auto)
Camera.GetDefaultCamera() is used to obtain the camera
camera.ApplySettingsAsync(BarcodeCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings) is called
dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync( is called with the camera
BarcodeCaptureSettings.Create() is used to construct settings (not new BarcodeCaptureSettings())
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
BarcodeCapture.Create(dataCaptureContext, settings) is used to create the mode (not BarcodeCapture.ForDataCaptureContext or new BarcodeCapture())
barcodeCapture.AddListener( is called (or barcodeCapture.BarcodeScanned += is wired)
DataCaptureView.Create( is used to create the view
BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Create(barcodeCapture, dataCaptureView) is called
The Activity implements IBarcodeCaptureListener (or subscribes to BarcodeScanned via EventHandler<BarcodeCaptureEventArgs>)
OnBarcodeScanned (or the BarcodeScanned event handler) reads session.NewlyRecognizedBarcode
OnSessionUpdated, OnObservationStarted, and OnObservationStopped are implemented if using the listener interface
barcodeCapture.Enabled = false is set inside OnBarcodeScanned
camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On) is called in OnResume (or equivalent)
camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.Off) is called in OnPause (or equivalent)
barcodeCapture.RemoveListener( is called in OnDestroy (or the event handler is unsubscribed)

evals.prompt: Add barcode scanning to my existing .NET Android Activity. Here's the Activity: EmptyActivity.cs. I want to scan QR codes and Data Matrix. When a barcode is scanned, log the barcode data.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates BarcodeCapture into EmptyActivity.cs with QR and Data Matrix symbologies enabled. OnBarcodeScanned logs the barcode data via Android.Util.Log.Debug or similar. Shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Symbology.Qr is enabled
Symbology.DataMatrix is enabled
No symbologies other than Qr and DataMatrix are enabled
BarcodeCapture.Create( is used
OnBarcodeScanned (or BarcodeScanned event) is implemented and accesses barcode.Data
The scan result is logged (Log.Debug, System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine, Console.WriteLine, or similar)
OnResume turns the camera on
OnPause turns the camera off
Setup checklist mentions Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my .NET Android BarcodeCapture app, I want to disable scanning, look up the barcode in a remote API (an async Task call), and re-enable scanning when the lookup completes. Wire this up safely.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.Enabled = false at the start of OnBarcodeScanned, awaits the async lookup, and re-enables scanning in a finally block. UI updates are dispatched via RunOnUiThread.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.Enabled = false is set inside OnBarcodeScanned (or the BarcodeScanned event handler) before the async work
An async/await call or a Task continuation is used to perform the lookup
barcodeCapture.Enabled = true is set after the lookup completes (typically in a finally block)
RunOnUiThread is used to dispatch UI work after the async lookup
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener/event) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration when a barcode is scanned in my .NET Android app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeCapture feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill reassigns barcodeCapture.Feedback.Success to a Feedback constructed with null vibration and null sound (or replaces the whole BarcodeCaptureFeedback with one whose Success has no audio or haptic effect).

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.Feedback.Success is assigned to a Feedback with no sound and no vibration (Feedback(vibration: null, sound: null) or equivalent)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a square viewfinder to my .NET Android BarcodeCapture preview so users can see where to aim.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RectangularViewfinder with RectangularViewfinderStyle.Square and assigns it to overlay.Viewfinder.

assertions.text: A RectangularViewfinder is constructed with RectangularViewfinderStyle.Square
The viewfinder is assigned to BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Viewfinder (overlay.Viewfinder = ...)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my .NET Android BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.CodeDuplicateFilter = TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance (using TimeSpan, not a bare integer, and not TimeInterval).

assertions.text: CodeDuplicateFilter is set to TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) (not a bare integer 500, not TimeInterval.Millis, not TimeInterval.millis)
CodeDuplicateFilter is set before BarcodeCapture.Create( or via barcodeCapture.ApplySettingsAsync
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Clean up my .NET Android BarcodeCapture integration properly when the Activity is destroyed — remove the listener and turn off the camera.
evals.expected_output: The skill ensures OnDestroy calls barcodeCapture.RemoveListener(this) (or unsubscribes the BarcodeScanned handler), and that OnPause calls camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.Off).

assertions.text: OnDestroy calls barcodeCapture.RemoveListener(this) or unsubscribes the BarcodeScanned/SessionUpdated event handlers
OnPause (or OnDestroy) calls camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.Off)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add an aimer-style viewfinder (the small Scandit aimer with a center dot) to my .NET Android BarcodeCapture preview so users aim at one code at a time. Here's my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill constructs a new AimerViewfinder and assigns it to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay's Viewfinder property, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: An AimerViewfinder is constructed with new AimerViewfinder()
The aimer viewfinder is assigned to BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Viewfinder (overlay.Viewfinder = ...)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Restrict barcode scanning to a centered rectangular region of the .NET Android preview (about 90% width, 30% height) so codes outside that band are ignored. Here's my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RectangularLocationSelection via RectangularLocationSelection.Create with a SizeWithUnit built from FloatWithUnit values in MeasureUnit.Fraction, assigns it to settings.LocationSelection, and applies the settings (before BarcodeCapture.Create or via ApplySettingsAsync).

assertions.text: A RectangularLocationSelection is created via RectangularLocationSelection.Create
A SizeWithUnit is built from FloatWithUnit values
MeasureUnit.Fraction is used for the relative size
The location selection is assigned to BarcodeCaptureSettings.LocationSelection (settings.LocationSelection = ...)
The settings are applied before BarcodeCapture.Create( or via barcodeCapture.ApplySettingsAsync
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want a circular aimer-driven scan zone in my .NET Android app: only accept the code closest to the center, within a radius. Use a radius-based location selection. Here's my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RadiusLocationSelection via RadiusLocationSelection.Create with a FloatWithUnit (MeasureUnit.Fraction) radius and assigns it to settings.LocationSelection. An AimerViewfinder is the recommended companion viewfinder.

assertions.text: A RadiusLocationSelection is created via RadiusLocationSelection.Create
The radius is a FloatWithUnit constructed with MeasureUnit.Fraction
The location selection is assigned to BarcodeCaptureSettings.LocationSelection (settings.LocationSelection = ...)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my .NET Android BarcodeCapture app I only want to accept barcodes whose data starts with "98". Other codes should be rejected and NOT trigger the success flow. Show the scanned barcode highlight as transparent for rejected codes. Here's my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: Inside OnBarcodeScanned the skill reads barcode.Data, and when it does not match the accept rule it sets the overlay brush to a transparent Brush (Brush.TransparentBrush or a transparent new Brush) and returns early without running the success handling. Matching codes proceed normally.

assertions.text: OnBarcodeScanned reads barcode.Data and tests it against the accept rule (starts with "98")
For a rejected code the overlay brush is set to a transparent Brush (overlay.Brush = Brush.TransparentBrush or a transparent new Brush(...))
(semantic) for a rejected code the handler returns early and does not run the accepted-code success flow
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Change the highlight drawn over recognized barcodes in my .NET Android app to a semi-transparent green fill with a solid green stroke. Here's my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns a new Brush(fillColor, strokeColor, strokeWidth) to BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Brush, building the colors with Color.FromRGBA.

assertions.text: A Brush is constructed via new Brush(fillColor, strokeColor, strokeWidth) (the three-argument Brush constructor)
The colors are built with Color.FromRGBA
The brush is assigned to BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Brush (overlay.Brush = ...)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Enable the full ASCII extension for Code 39 in my .NET Android BarcodeCapture settings so lowercase and control characters decode. Here's my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill obtains the per-symbology settings via settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code39) and calls SetExtensionEnabled("full_ascii", true), then applies the settings.

assertions.text: settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code39) is used to obtain the per-symbology settings
SetExtensionEnabled("full_ascii", true) is called on the Code39 symbology settings
The settings are applied before BarcodeCapture.Create( or via barcodeCapture.ApplySettingsAsync
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Require the Mod 43 optional checksum for Code 39 scans in my .NET Android app to reduce misreads. Here's my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill obtains settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code39) and sets its Checksums property to Checksum.Mod43, then applies the settings.

assertions.text: settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code39) is used to obtain the per-symbology settings
The Checksums property is set to Checksum.Mod43 (code39.Checksums = Checksum.Mod43)
The settings are applied before BarcodeCapture.Create( or via barcodeCapture.ApplySettingsAsync
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: My .NET Android app scans Code 128 codes that are exactly 6, 7, or 8 symbols long — restrict the active symbol counts so nothing else decodes. Here's my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill obtains settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code128) and assigns its ActiveSymbolCounts to a HashSet<short> containing 6, 7, 8, then applies the settings.

assertions.text: settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code128) is used to obtain the per-symbology settings
ActiveSymbolCounts is assigned a HashSet<short> (or other ICollection<short>) containing 6, 7, and 8
The short values 6, 7, 8 are typed as short, not int (e.g. new HashSet<short>(new short[] { 6, 7, 8 }))
The settings are applied before BarcodeCapture.Create( or via barcodeCapture.ApplySettingsAsync
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Some of our QR labels are printed light-on-dark (color inverted). Enable decoding of color-inverted QR codes in my .NET Android BarcodeCapture app. Here's my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables Symbology.Qr, obtains settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Qr) and sets ColorInvertedEnabled = true, then applies the settings.

assertions.text: settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Qr) is used to obtain the per-symbology settings
ColorInvertedEnabled is set to true (qr.ColorInvertedEnabled = true)
(semantic) the QR symbology itself remains enabled in addition to color-inverted decoding
The settings are applied before BarcodeCapture.Create( or via barcodeCapture.ApplySettingsAsync
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to scan GS1 Composite codes (a linear code with a 2D composite component) in my .NET Android app. Enable composite types A, B, and C. Here's my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables the composite types both by calling settings.EnableSymbologies(CompositeType.A | CompositeType.B | CompositeType.C) (to enable the required component symbologies) and by setting settings.EnabledCompositeTypes to the same CompositeType bit flag, then applies the settings.

assertions.text: settings.EnableSymbologies(CompositeType.A | CompositeType.B | CompositeType.C) is called to enable the required component symbologies
settings.EnabledCompositeTypes is set to CompositeType.A | CompositeType.B | CompositeType.C
The settings are applied before BarcodeCapture.Create( or via barcodeCapture.ApplySettingsAsync
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved
